_doc: Daily prospective model-run output. Each runs[] entry is one analyst run (one calendar day). Each event is one in-scope CVE scored against NP+DI+DQ+Hacker. Maintained by the analyst agent (6:10am daily). Mirrors the schema documented in agents/analyst-prompt.md §1a.

events.dq_rationale: NP+DI raw — no DQ rescue needed
events.hacker_rationale: Default-config + network-edge + primitive-direct path traversal; paired with -1709 auth bypass = unauth RCE
events.tier_anchor: Spring4Shell-class unauth RCE primitive in vendor admin console
events.anchor_note: New KEV addition 2026-04-28. SlashAndGrab cluster precedent (Feb 2024 ransomware campaign). Likely retroactive KEV listing.

events.hacker_rationale: Default-config escape function flaw + network-edge HTTP API + primitive-direct query manipulation; auth varies by app
events.tier_anchor: Log4Shell-shape injection in HTTP-facing trust boundary
events.anchor_note: Spring security disclosure 2026-04-27 (5-CVE Spring AI batch). Added to watch list today.

events.hacker_rationale: SQL injection in delete path; default-config + network-edge + primitive-direct exfil/destroy
events.tier_anchor: classic SQLi in framework-managed query builder
events.anchor_note: Same Spring AI batch as -40967. Fix 1.0.6 / 1.1.5.

events.dq_rationale: NP+DI raw — heap overflow via crafted Destination header is DI by trust-boundary rule
events.hacker_rationale: Network-edge + primitive-direct RCE potential, but DAV module is NOT default-on in nginx — narrow exposure
events.tier_anchor: default-off feature-module heap bug, similar to mod-specific vulns we've seen in Apache
events.anchor_note: Heap overflow via integer underflow in ngx_http_dav_copy_move_handler. Researcher-assisted (calif.io); not Mythos-attributed.

events.dq_rationale: NP+DI raw — CWE-502 deser is canonical DI
events.hacker_rationale: Default-config + primitive-direct deser RCE; not network-edge by default (research framework, not exposed product)
events.tier_anchor: deser RCE in research/ML framework — primitive is real but blast radius is narrow
events.anchor_note: Companion CVE -24165 (same pattern). NVIDIA is a Glasswing participant — flagged as probable participant self-scan.
